short_description_of_the_course.label: Short Description of the Course
short_description_of_the_course.value: This course is an introduction to quantum computing, covering theory and practice (through the use of simulation software). The main topics are: quantum information processing; quantum protocols for communication (teleportation, dense coding, error correction) and cryptography (key distribution); quantum algorithms (Deutsch’s algorithm, Grover’s algorithm, overview of Shor’s algorithm); quantum annealing

timetable_and_length_and_frequency_of_te.label: Timetable and length and frequency of teaching sessions
timetable_and_length_and_frequency_of_te.value: 2 hours of lecture time and 1 hour of tutorial or practical work, per week

course_aims.label: Course Aims
course_aims.value: The aim of the course is to introduce the theory and practice of quantum computing.

intended_learning_outcomes_of_course.label: Intended Learning Outcomes of Course
intended_learning_outcomes_of_course.value:
By the end of this course students will be able to:
Explain the principles of quantum computation and the characteristics that distinguish it from classical computation.
Calculate the effect of quantum protocols and algorithms according to the laws of Hilbert spaces.
Use the notation of quantum circuit diagrams to define and understand quantum protocols and algorithms.
Understand specific quantum algorithms and protocols and be able to evaluate them on example data: Deutsch’s algorithm, Grover’s algorithm, quantum teleportation, quantum dense coding, the BB84 key distribution protocol, at least one error-correction protocol.
Express and work with quantum algorithms and protocols in a standard simulation environment, e.g. Qiskit
Explain the principles of quantum annealing and its key differences from algorithmic quantum computing.
Use a standard quantum annealing framework (e.g. D-Wave) to model and solve standard optimisation problems.

description_of_summative_assessment.label: Description of Summative Assessment
description_of_summative_assessment.value:
80% for the end-of-year exam
20% for assessed coursework, which is likely to be separated into two smaller assignments

The assessed coursework will assess ILOs 5 and 7
The exam will assess ILOs 1, 2, 3, 4 and 6

formative_assessment_feedback.label: Formative Assessment & Feedback
formative_assessment_feedback.value:
Individual written feedback will be provided on the assessed exercises.
Generic feedback will be provided to the class, on the assessed exercises, during lecture time.
Generic feedback will be provided to the class, via Moodle, on the final exam.
